touches.ai_summary: Cold email sent to Nathan Stern introducing our onboarding automation platform. No response received.
Follow-up cold email to Nathan Stern highlighting ROI case studies for mid-market CS teams. No response received.
LinkedIn message sent to Nathan Stern referencing ObsidianBridge's recent growth and onboarding challenges. No response received.
Brief cold call with Nathan Stern. He acknowledged receiving prior outreach and expressed interest in learning more about onboarding automation. Agreed to follow-up email with calendar link.

questions_asked.question: What makes your platform different from what we're already evaluating?
questions_asked.answer: Our AI-driven automation specifically targets onboarding workflows, with native integrations for CS teams rather than generic sales tools.

next_steps: Send calendar link for qualification call

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with calendar link and brief overview of onboarding automation capabilities. Nathan responded confirming interest and scheduled qualification call.

questions_asked.question: Can you share pricing ballpark before we meet?
questions_asked.answer: Typically ranges $600-$1200 per seat annually depending on package. Happy to discuss specifics on our call.

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call with Nathan Stern. Discussed current onboarding process, pain points with manual workflows, and team size. Nathan confirmed 35-person CS team and interest in automating repetitive onboarding tasks. Mentioned they're also looking at Gong.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the urgency to solve this now?
questions_asked.answer: We've doubled headcount in 6 months and our manual processes don't scale. Onboarding takes 4-6 weeks per customer.

questions_asked.question: How does your platform compare to Gong for onboarding use cases?
questions_asked.answer: Gong focuses on conversation intelligence for sales. We're purpose-built for CS automation and onboarding workflows.

next_steps: Schedule discovery call with broader context

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Nathan Stern. Deep dive into ObsidianBridge's onboarding workflow, integration requirements, and success metrics. Nathan shared that CFO is pushing for automation ROI this quarter. Discussed team structure and identified Leo Gomez (COO) as executive sponsor.

questions_asked.question: Who else needs to be involved in evaluating this solution?
questions_asked.answer: Leo Gomez, our COO, owns CS operations. Sophia Mensah in RevOps will need to validate integrations. Michael Flores handles procurement.

questions_asked.question: What's your current timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: Ideally before end of Q2. We need something in place before our summer customer surge.

questions_asked.question: Can your platform integrate with our existing CRM and ticketing systems?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native integrations with major CRMs and support API connections for custom ticketing platforms.

objections: Budget approval might be tight this quarter given other CS investments
next_steps: Schedule follow-up to discuss technical requirements

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call with Nathan to review technical requirements and integration points. Discussed API capabilities and data security protocols. Nathan confirmed ObsidianBridge uses Salesforce and Zendesk.

questions_asked.question: What's your data retention and security posture?
questions_asked.answer: SOC 2 Type II certified, data encrypted at rest and in transit, configurable retention policies from 1-7 years.

questions_asked.question: Are there any compliance requirements we should be aware of?
questions_asked.answer: We're subject to standard enterprise security audits. Nothing beyond SOC 2 for now.

next_steps: Send technical spec sheet to Nathan for Sophia's review
Schedule next call to discuss use case prioritization

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call with Nathan focusing on use case prioritization. Discussed which onboarding workflows to automate first and expected ROI. Nathan mentioned Gong demo went well but they're more interested in CS-specific features.

questions_asked.question: What would a successful first 90 days look like with our platform?
questions_asked.answer: Automating our initial onboarding email sequence and reducing time-to-first-value from 6 weeks to 3 weeks.

questions_asked.question: Can we start with a subset of seats and scale up?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely. Many customers start with 10-20 seats and expand based on adoption.

next_steps: Prepare demo tailored to onboarding automation use case
Schedule discovery call to finalize requirements

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Nathan with demo prep questionnaire and calendar link. Nathan responded confirming key workflows to demonstrate and requested inclusion of Sophia Mensah in upcoming calls.
next_steps: Demo scheduled with Nathan and Sophia

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Nathan to finalize demo requirements and confirm attendees. Discussed specific onboarding workflows to showcase and integration points with Salesforce. Nathan confirmed Leo Gomez will want to see ROI projections before final decision.

questions_asked.question: What metrics does Leo care most about for CS automation?
questions_asked.answer: Time to onboard, customer satisfaction scores, and CS team efficiency. He's very ROI-focused.

questions_asked.question: Will we be able to customize the workflows during the trial?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, our trial includes full configuration access with implementation support.

next_steps: Schedule demo with Nathan and Sophia

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email confirming demo scheduled for next week. Shared pre-demo overview of platform capabilities and onboarding automation framework. Nathan acknowledged receipt.

next_steps: Demo prepared with Salesforce integration focus

touches.ai_summary: Demo with Nathan and Sophia (rescheduled from earlier date). Walked through onboarding automation workflows, Salesforce integration, and ROI projections. Sophia asked detailed questions about data mapping and API limits. Nathan expressed strong interest and mentioned pricing will be key factor vs. Gong.

questions_asked.question: What are the API rate limits for Salesforce sync?
questions_asked.answer: We batch sync every 15 minutes with configurable throttling. Typical 35-seat deployment uses ~20% of Salesforce API limits.

questions_asked.question: Can we customize the onboarding templates?
questions_asked.answer: Fully customizable through our template builder with drag-and-drop interface.

questions_asked.question: What concerns do you have about implementation timeline?
questions_asked.answer: We need to go live before June. Can you guarantee 30-day implementation?

objections: Pricing needs to be competitive with Gong's proposal

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation session with Nathan and Sophia. Deep dive into API documentation, data security protocols, and integration architecture. Sophia tested sandbox environment and validated Salesforce connector. Both expressed satisfaction with technical capabilities.

questions_asked.question: How do you handle data conflicts during sync?
questions_asked.answer: Configurable conflict resolution with options for source-of-truth prioritization and manual review workflows.

questions_asked.question: What level of support comes with implementation?
questions_asked.answer: Dedicated implementation manager, weekly check-ins, and 24/7 technical support during first 60 days.

questions_asked.question: Are there any technical blockers we should address before proposal?
questions_asked.answer: No blockers. We'll need Michael from procurement involved for contracting.

next_steps: Schedule exec meeting with Leo Gomez

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Nathan with technical evaluation summary and next steps. Requested introduction to Leo Gomez for executive alignment meeting. Nathan responded positively and offered to facilitate intro.

questions_asked.question: What should we prepare for the Leo meeting?
questions_asked.answer: Focus on ROI, implementation timeline, and strategic value. He's data-driven and appreciates concise business cases.

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting with Leo Gomez and Nathan Stern. Presented ROI analysis showing 40% reduction in onboarding time and $180K annual efficiency savings. Leo asked about competitive positioning vs. Gong and implementation risk. Expressed concern about budget allocation for Q2.

questions_asked.question: Why should we choose you over Gong?
questions_asked.answer: Gong is sales-focused conversation intelligence. We're purpose-built for CS onboarding automation with deeper workflow customization.

questions_asked.question: What's the risk if implementation runs over 30 days?
questions_asked.answer: We guarantee 30-day go-live or first month free. 95% of deployments complete on time.

objections: Budget is tight this quarter. Need to see competitive pricing.
next_steps: Schedule follow-up exec meeting to discuss proposal

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up executive meeting with Leo Gomez and Nathan. Discussed pricing structure and package options. Leo pushed for aggressive discount citing Gong's lower pricing. Reviewed automations_plus_consulting package benefits and implementation support. Leo requested formal proposal with best pricing.

questions_asked.question: Can you match Gong's pricing for 35 seats?
questions_asked.answer: Gong's pricing reflects different capabilities. Our CS-specific features justify premium, but we can discuss creative packaging.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for final decision?
questions_asked.answer: Need proposal by end of week. Decision by mid-month to stay on track for Q2 budget.

questions_asked.question: What's included in the consulting package?
questions_asked.answer: Dedicated CSM, quarterly strategy reviews, custom workflow development, and priority support.

objections: Gong quoted significantly lower for similar seat count
next_steps: Prepare formal proposal with competitive pricing

touches.ai_summary: Proposal call with Nathan Stern. Reviewed formal proposal for 35 seats at $835/seat ($29,200 ACV) with automations_plus_consulting package. Nathan appreciated the discount but mentioned Leo is still comparing to Gong. Discussed implementation timeline and success metrics.

questions_asked.question: Is this your best pricing?
questions_asked.answer: We've structured this competitively with 21.6% discount off list price given your strategic fit and timeline.

questions_asked.question: What would make this a clear yes for Leo?
questions_asked.answer: Pricing closer to Gong's proposal and confidence in 30-day implementation.

touches.ai_summary: Brief pricing call with Nathan. Discussed potential pricing adjustments and package trade-offs. Nathan mentioned Leo is considering Gong's lower pricing despite preferring our CS capabilities. Agreed to explore options.

questions_asked.question: Can you go lower on per-seat pricing?

touches.ai_summary: Pricing call with Nathan (rescheduled from earlier date). Reviewed alternative pricing scenarios including core automation vs. consulting package trade-offs. Nathan confirmed Leo wants to stick with consulting package but needs better pricing to justify vs. Gong. Discussed payment terms flexibility.

questions_asked.question: Can we do quarterly payments instead of annual?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can structure quarterly billing with 5% adjustment to annual price.

questions_asked.question: Is Gong's proposal for equivalent capabilities?
questions_asked.answer: Different feature set. Theirs is sales-focused, ours is CS onboarding. Leo sees value in our approach but price is a sticking point.

objections: Leo needs to justify premium over Gong to CFO

touches.ai_summary: Pricing call with Nathan to finalize terms. Confirmed $29,200 ACV at $835/seat with automations_plus_consulting package. Nathan committed to recommending this to Leo. Discussed contract structure and implementation kickoff timeline.

questions_asked.question: Can we include expansion terms for adding seats mid-year?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can add pro-rated pricing terms for mid-year expansion at same per-seat rate.

questions_asked.question: What's Leo's biggest remaining concern?
questions_asked.answer: Ensuring we deliver on the 30-day implementation promise. That's critical for our summer timeline.

next_steps: Nathan to present to Leo for final approval

touches.ai_summary: Final negotiation call with Nathan. Reviewed revised contract with 60-day cancellation notice and confirmed all terms. Nathan confirmed Leo approved the deal pending legal review. Michael Flores joining for procurement sign-off. Target signature date end of week.

questions_asked.question: What's the process after signature for implementation kickoff?
questions_asked.answer: Implementation manager assigned within 48 hours, kickoff call scheduled within first week, go-live target 30 days from kickoff.

questions_asked.question: Any remaining concerns from Leo or Michael?
